description:
Reported by a Jira-sync plugin author. Task has updated_at (src/task.rs:133) but nothing plugin-facing that records when a given task was last successfully synced by a plugin. Bidirectional conflict resolution on updated_at currently forces plugins to maintain their own side-cache under auth-dir.

Proposal: add synced_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>> (or a per-plugin map) to Task, written by balls after a successful push/sync response is applied. Expose it on the Task JSON sent to plugins during push/sync so they can compare against their remote's updated_at without a side-cache. Forward-compat passthrough on Task (see commit 49d30ec) means adding the field won't break older data.

Design questions to settle during implementation:
- single synced_at or a map keyed by plugin name? (Multi-plugin users need the map.)
- when exactly does it update — on push ack, on sync ack, both?
- does a rejected/failed sync clear or leave it alone?

Deliverables: task struct change, runner.rs update site, README spec update, unit + integration tests.
title: Expose per-task last-synced timestamp to plugins
